edible_parts: Seeds, young leaves, and tuberous rhizomes (all cooked/eaten).
other_uses: Spectacular aquatic with huge blue-green leaves held above the water and pale-yellow flowers.
prep: The nutlike seeds are eaten raw or cooked, young leaves cooked as greens, and the starchy rhizomes cooked like potato — a major Indigenous food. A striking pond plant that spreads vigorously.
soil: Aquatic; still or slow water to ~6 ft deep, mucky bottoms.
risks: Spreads aggressively in still water; give it room.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
